Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue The Trial Date And Re-Open Discovery For The Limited Purpose Of Compelling Plaintiffs Independent Medical Examination
Off Calendar Aug-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Duncan Lemmon and Megan Van Arsdale of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party RPI Company
Johannes C. Demarzi of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Clarence Brown Jr.

RULING:
DEFENDANT RPI COMPANY'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE AND RE-OPEN DISCOVERY FOR THE LIMITED PURPOSE OF COMPELLING PLAINTIFF'S INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION

Prior to the hearing the parties stipulated that the same argument can be used in the Brown case (#274113) and the Smith case (#274178). Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant RPI Company's motion to continue the trial date and re-open discovery for the limited purpose of compelling plaintiff's independent medical examination is denied. In addition to failing to meet the requirements of C.C.P. 2032.310(b), defendant failed to demonstrate good cause to compel a second medical exam of plaintiff. (C.C.P. 2032.320(a).) Defendant failed to make an affirmative showing of good cause why the trial date should be continued, as required by CRC 3.1332. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kyle McLean of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13787 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
